title: The Learning Roadmap
subtitle: Your 5-phase journey from beginner to confident backend developer

sections.title: Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-4)
sections.text:
Build the core mental model of how the web works. Don't touch frameworks yet — understand the raw machinery.

Goals:

  • Understand DNS, TCP/IP, and HTTP request-response cycle
  • Build a raw HTTP server in your chosen language (Node.js http module, Python http.server, Go net/http)
  • Understand what the server loop does (listen, accept, read, process, write, close)
  • Understand client-server architecture and the difference between frontend and backend
  • Learn basic Linux commands (navigate, read files, manage processes)

Project: Build a simple CLI tool that fetches a URL and prints the response headers and body.

events.title: Week 3
events.desc: Build raw HTTP server from scratch

sections.caption: Phase 1: Understand the raw machinery before touching any framework

sections.title: Phase 2: Core (Weeks 5-10)
sections.text:
Build real applications with databases, APIs, and authentication. This is where you stop being a beginner.

Goals:

  • Learn SQL and relational databases (PostgreSQL recommended)
  • Build a REST API with Express (Node), FastAPI (Python), or Gin (Go)
  • Implement user registration and login with bcrypt + JWT
  • Understand layered architecture (Controller → Service → Repository)
  • Add basic caching with Redis

Project: Build a task management API with user auth, task CRUD, categories, and search.

events.title: Week 7-8
events.desc: Build REST API with auth + JWT

sections.caption: Phase 2: Build real applications with databases, APIs, and auth
sections.code:
# Phase 2 project: Task Manager API

POST /api/auth/register   # Create account
POST /api/auth/login      # Get JWT
GET  /api/tasks           # List user's tasks
POST /api/tasks           # Create task
PUT  /api/tasks/:id       # Update task
DELETE /api/tasks/:id     # Delete task

# With layered architecture:
# controllers/  → HTTP handlers
# services/     → Business logic
# repositories/ → Database queries
# middleware/   → Auth, logging, validation

sections.title: Phase 3: Scale (Weeks 11-16)
sections.text:
Learn to build applications that handle real traffic with multiple users, security concerns, and team collaboration.

Goals:

  • Learn Git branching strategies and PR workflow
  • Write unit, integration, and E2E tests
  • Understand security: SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, rate limiting
  • Learn Docker for containerization
  • Understand load balancing and horizontal scaling concepts

Project: Containerize your task manager with Docker, add rate limiting, write tests, and deploy to a VPS.
sections.caption: Phase 3: Containerize, test, secure, and deploy to production
sections.code:
# Docker Compose for your task manager
version: '3'
services:
  app:
    build: .
    ports:
      - '3000:3000'
    depends_on:
      - db
      - redis
  db:
    image: postgres:16
    volumes:
      - pgdata:/var/lib/postgresql/data
  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    
volumes:
  pgdata:

sections.title: Phase 4: DevOps & Architecture (Weeks 17-20)
sections.text:
Level up from developer to engineer. Learn about system design, monitoring, and advanced deployment strategies.

Goals:

  • Understand architecture patterns (monolith vs microservices, event-driven)
  • Learn CI/CD pipelines (GitHub Actions)
  • Understand monitoring, logging, and alerting
  • Learn a cloud platform (AWS, GCP, or Azure basics)
  • Study system design fundamentals (caching, database sharding, CDN)

Project: Set up a CI/CD pipeline that automatically deploys your Dockerized app on every push. Add monitoring with Prometheus + Grafana.
sections.caption: Phase 4: CI/CD pipelines, cloud deployment, and system design thinking

sections.title: Phase 5: Mastery & Beyond
sections.text:
At this point, you have the foundation to learn anything in the backend world. The specifics (language, framework, cloud provider) matter less than the mental models you've built.

What's next:

  • Deep dive — Go deeper into one area: distributed systems, database internals, networking, security
  • Contribute — Open source contributions to backend projects you use
  • Teach — Write blog posts, create tutorials, mentor others. Teaching solidifies knowledge.
  • Build — Launch your own SaaS product. There's no better teacher than running a real production system.

The big takeaway: Every server, API, database, and deployment follows the same fundamental patterns we've covered. Frameworks come and go. The fundamentals are forever.

items.heading: Deep Dive
items.body: Distributed systems, DB internals, networking protocols, advanced security.

items.heading: Contribute
items.body: Open source contributions to backend projects. Real-world code reviews.

items.heading: Teach
items.body: Blog posts, tutorials, mentoring. Teaching is the best way to learn deeply.

items.heading: Build
items.body: Launch your own product. Production experience teaches what tutorials can't.

sections.caption: The journey never ends — deep dive, contribute, teach, and build
sections.callout: The backend landscape changes every year, but the fundamentals stay the same. DNS, TCP/IP, HTTP, databases, caching, security, and deployment — master these and you can learn any framework in a week.
